contract.description: First narrow seed slice around pinch singularities for literature-facing prerequisite queries.

evidence.note: Initial hand-authored slice for the pinch-singularities batch.

dependencies.failure_mode_if_absent: Cannot parse which kind of singularity a paper is referring to when it contrasts poles with cut structure.
dependencies.rationale: Recognizing propagator singularities in papers requires distinguishing isolated pole structure from branch structure.

evidence.note: Initial hand-authored slice for the pinch-singularities batch.

dependencies.failure_mode_if_absent: Cannot identify what the pinch is a singularity of or why propagators are central to the discussion.
dependencies.rationale: A pinch singularity is recognized as a situation in which propagator singularities constrain contour motion.

evidence.note: Initial hand-authored slice for the pinch-singularities batch.

dependencies.failure_mode_if_absent: Cannot follow why the contour cannot be moved away from the singular region.
dependencies.rationale: Standard pinch arguments are phrased in terms of failed contour deformations.

evidence.note: Initial hand-authored slice for the pinch-singularities batch.

dependencies.failure_mode_if_absent: Cannot distinguish which singular structures can actually pinch the contour in a standard analytic argument.
dependencies.rationale: Pinch discussions rely on identifying which singular structures can trap a contour and how isolated poles differ from branch behavior.

evidence.note: Initial hand-authored slice for the pinch-singularities batch.

dependencies.failure_mode_if_absent: Cannot follow why propagator poles sit on opposite sides of the integration contour in the standard argument.
dependencies.rationale: The Feynman prescription determines how propagator poles are displaced and therefore how contour arguments are read.

evidence.note: Initial hand-authored slice for the pinch-singularities batch.

dependencies.failure_mode_if_absent: Cannot follow the standard pinch argument based on the relative position of propagator singularities.
dependencies.rationale: Standard pinch-singularity reasoning proceeds by tracking how propagator singularities approach and trap the contour.

nodes.label: Contour deformation
nodes.summary: Standard complex-analysis procedure of continuously moving an integration contour while tracking which singularities obstruct the motion.

nodes.label: Poles vs branch points
nodes.summary: Distinction between isolated pole singularities and branch-point or cut singularities in complex analysis.

nodes.label: Feynman i epsilon prescription
nodes.summary: Prescription that displaces propagator poles off the real axis to define causal contour choices in relativistic QFT.

nodes.label: Pinch singularities
nodes.summary: Singular configurations in which contour deformations are obstructed by competing singularities that trap the contour.

nodes.label: Propagator singularities
nodes.summary: Singular structure contributed by propagator denominators, especially the location and interpretation of their poles.
